compartments.label: Cytosol
compartments.notes: Shared soluble phase and metabolic field.

compartments.label: Smooth ER
compartments.notes: Lipid metabolism, Ca storage, CYP detox.

definition.notes: Mixed-authority hepatocyte definition: structural contracts are source-backed where stated; relative 0-1 pools and organelle cycles are schematic and cannot drive quantitative validation.

hepatocyte_radius_um.name: hepatocyte_radius_um
hepatocyte_radius_um.notes: Equivalent-sphere radius derived geometrically from the 3400 um3 reference volume; not a claim that a polarized hepatocyte is spherical.

initial_pool_unit.name: initial_pool_unit
initial_pool_unit.notes: M015 uses normalized pools until quantitative hepatocyte concentrations are curated.

pools.label: ATP
pools.notes: Energy currency available to ATP-costly processes.

pools.label: Intracellular bile acids
pools.notes: Intracellular hepatocyte bile-acid pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.

pools.label: Canalicular bile acids
pools.notes: Mass-conserving destination for BSEP export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.

pools.label: Intracellular bilirubin conjugates
pools.notes: Intracellular conjugated-bilirubin pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.

pools.label: Canalicular bilirubin conjugates
pools.notes: Mass-conserving destination for MRP2 export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.

pools.label: Potassium
pools.notes: Dominant intracellular cation (~140 mM); sets resting potential.

pools.label: Sodium
pools.notes: Low inside (~12 mM); its gradient powers secondary transport.

pools.label: Magnesium
pools.notes: Free Mg2+ (~0.5 mM); cofactor for ATP-dependent enzymes.

pools.label: GTP
pools.notes: Guanine nucleotide pool for GTPases and biosynthesis.

pools.label: NADP+
pools.notes: Oxidised NADP pool; kept small so NADPH/NADP+ stays high.

pools.label: Oxygen
pools.notes: Cytosolic oxygen availability for mitochondrial and peroxisomal oxidation.

pools.label: Folded secretory cargo
pools.notes: ER quality-control-passed cargo ready for Golgi processing.

pools.label: Autophagy cargo
pools.notes: Damaged protein/organelle material committed to autophagy.

alberts_mbc.notes: Broad cell biology reference for organelle function and cross-organelle stress responses.
alberts_mbc.title: Molecular Biology of the Cell, NCBI Bookshelf

cell_biology_by_numbers.notes: Quantitative reference for sizes, concentrations, copy numbers, and order-of-magnitude checks.
cell_biology_by_numbers.title: Cell Biology by the Numbers

human_cell_proteome_quantitative_2023.notes: Reports a characteristic average hepatocyte volume of 3400 um3 and discusses cell-volume scaling of protein content.
human_cell_proteome_quantitative_2023.title: Quantitative Aspects of the Human Cell Proteome

project_roadmap_07.notes: Project-specific hepatocyte-first engine boundary and stochastic modeling contract.
project_roadmap_07.title: Integrated Cell Engine Roadmap

stochastic_policy.notes: M015 defines the contract; event kinetics are implemented in later process modules.

validation_targets.description: Definition includes sinusoidal and canalicular membrane regions.
Every stochastic event must be described as a hazard tied to cell/organelle state.

reference_ranges.notes: Energy currency available to ATP-costly processes.
Intracellular hepatocyte bile-acid pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.
Mass-conserving destination for BSEP export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.
Intracellular conjugated-bilirubin pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.
Mass-conserving destination for MRP2 export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.
Dominant intracellular cation (~140 mM); sets resting potential.
Low inside (~12 mM); its gradient powers secondary transport.
Free Mg2+ (~0.5 mM); cofactor for ATP-dependent enzymes.
Guanine nucleotide pool for GTPases and biosynthesis.
Oxidised NADP pool; kept small so NADPH/NADP+ stays high.
Cytosolic oxygen availability for mitochondrial and peroxisomal oxidation.
ER quality-control-passed cargo ready for Golgi processing.
Damaged protein/organelle material committed to autophagy.
Equivalent-sphere radius derived geometrically from the 3400 um3 reference volume; not a claim that a polarized hepatocyte is spherical.

cellular_response.notes: Intracellular-to-canalicular export is mass-conserving. Surface activity is a relative experimental input, not a turnover rate. CYP7A1 feedback and basolateral escape remain absent because the evidence panel does not identify transferable absolute rates. Damage exposure is stress-time (s), not a lesion count. Fate is evidence-only until a matched temporal commitment calibration is supplied.

supported_by: quiescent G0 maintained; no cell-cycle commitment

timing_profile.label: rat hepatocyte post-PHx first-cycle timing
timing_profile.notes: Rat PHx anchor: S phase begins around 18 h and DNA synthesis peaks 21-24 h. S/M durations use generic mammalian anchors until hepatocyte-specific phase durations are added.

events.text: Initialized mixed-authority hepatocyte snapshot; relative pools and organelle cycles are schematic.
Cargo misfolded_secretory_protein lost on er_quality_control->proteasome: success_probability=0.269, edge_stress=0.032, quality=0.240.
Cargo canalicular_bile_transporter retained on golgi->canalicular_face: success_probability=0.601, edge_stress=0.057, quality=0.900.
Cargo lysosomal_hydrolase retained on er_quality_control->golgi: success_probability=0.602, edge_stress=0.045, quality=0.880.

atp_death_switch.notes: The established apoptosis module owns irreversible apoptosis-versus-necrosis commitment.
atp_death_switch.title: Intracellular ATP is a switch in the decision between apoptosis and necrosis

bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.notes: Hydrophobic bile-acid exposure in freshly isolated rat hepatocytes caused ROS, mitochondrial permeability transition, and apoptosis.
bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.title: Bile acid-induced rat hepatocyte apoptosis is inhibited by antioxidants and MPT blockers

bsep_cholestasis.notes: Loss of BSEP function changes hepatocyte bile-acid disposition and sensitizes to cholestatic injury.
bsep_cholestasis.title: Disruption of BSEP Function in HepaRG Cells Alters Bile Acid Disposition

cholestasis_er_stress.notes: Reduced bile-acid transporter expression in hepatocytes produces intrahepatic cholestasis with ER stress.
cholestasis_er_stress.title: Hepatocyte-specific ablation of Foxa2 alters bile acid homeostasis and results in ER stress

expression_unknown_policy.notes: Gene-specific RNA and protein kinetics remain null until measured or calibrated; generic demo rates cannot silently become hepatocyte parameters.
expression_unknown_policy.title: Genome-expression milestone missing-data policy

genomic_burst_kinetics.notes: Mammalian alleles transcribe in bursts; enhancer and core-promoter architecture affect burst frequency and size.
genomic_burst_kinetics.title: Genomic encoding of transcriptional burst kinetics

gtex_v8.notes: Bulk tissue reference; liver values are not single-hepatocyte molecule counts and require cell-composition-aware interpretation.
gtex_v8.title: NIH Genotype-Tissue Expression project, data release V8

hcv_epigenetic_scar.notes: A defined exposure-specific persistent liver epigenetic scar; not a generic stress-memory rate.
hcv_epigenetic_scar.title: HCV-induced epigenetic changes persist after sustained virologic response

hepatocyte_regeneration_cycle.notes: Mature hepatocytes are quiescent and can re-enter the cell cycle after priming and mitogenic signaling.
hepatocyte_regeneration_cycle.title: Cellular and Molecular Basis of Liver Regeneration

hmdb_2022.notes: Curated human blood/plasma concentration reference ranges; specimen compartment is retained explicitly.
hmdb_2022.title: HMDB 5.0: the Human Metabolome Database for 2022

human_bile_acid_death_mode.notes: High GCDC exposure produced predominantly oncotic necrosis in primary human hepatocytes and HepaRG, unlike apoptosis-dominant rat responses.
human_bile_acid_death_mode.title: Mechanisms of bile acid toxicity in human and rat hepatocytes

human_bsep_taurocholate_2013.notes: Taurocholate transport by recombinant human BSEP in inverted Sf9 membrane vesicles.
human_bsep_taurocholate_2013.title: Early identification of clinically relevant drug interactions with the human bile salt export pump

human_cyp7a1_feedback.notes: Primary human hepatocyte data support bile-acid-dependent CYP7A1 repression and show that human regulation is not reducible to a single FXR-SHP edge.
human_cyp7a1_feedback.title: Bile acids and cytokines inhibit the human cholesterol 7-alpha-hydroxylase gene via the JNK/c-Jun pathway

human_fxr_shp_regulation.notes: Supports human FXR-to-SHP regulation and the SHP/LRH-1 links to CYP7A1 and NTCP regulation.
human_fxr_shp_regulation.title: Human FXR regulates SHP expression through direct binding to an LRH-1 binding site

human_hepatic_transit_1996.notes: Tracer transit-time model fitted in eight normal volunteers; reports portal flow fraction and liver mean transit time.
human_hepatic_transit_1996.title: An anatomic and physiological model of hepatic vascular system

human_hepatocyte_proteome_2016.notes: Quantitative LC-MS/MS of purified hepatocytes from seven human liver donors using Total Protein Approach and Proteomic Ruler.
human_hepatocyte_proteome_2016.title: In-depth quantitative analysis and comparison of the human hepatocyte and hepatoma cell line HepG2 proteomes

human_hepatocyte_renewal.notes: Retrospective carbon-14 birth dating; population renewal is not a fixed single-cell lifespan.
human_hepatocyte_renewal.title: Diploid hepatocytes drive physiological liver renewal in adult humans

human_hepatocyte_somatic_mutations.notes: Somatic sequence variants are stable physical records; the model does not infer an individual variant without data.
human_hepatocyte_somatic_mutations.title: Single-cell analysis of somatic mutations in human hepatocytes

human_hnf4a_cyp7a1.notes: Supports HNF4A as a major positive CYP7A1 transcriptional regulator and parallel FXR/SHP and FGF19 feedback mechanisms.
human_hnf4a_cyp7a1.title: Retinoic acid represses CYP7A1 expression in human hepatocytes and HepG2 cells

human_liver_adenylates_1992.notes: HPLC measurements in control human liver tissue: ATP, ADP, AMP, total adenine nucleotides, energy charge and NAD+.
human_liver_adenylates_1992.title: Can adenine nucleotides predict primary nonfunction of the human liver homograft?

human_liver_atp_synthesis_2008.notes: In-vivo 31P magnetization-transfer measurements in nine healthy volunteers; the reported rate is apparent Pi-to-ATP exchange.
human_liver_atp_synthesis_2008.title: Quantitative ATP synthesis in human liver measured by localized 31P spectroscopy using the magnetization transfer experiment

human_liver_cell_atlas.notes: Single-cell RNA-seq of about 10,000 cells from nine human donors supports donor-aware hepatocyte heterogeneity and zonation.
human_liver_cell_atlas.title: A human liver cell atlas reveals heterogeneity and epithelial progenitors

human_liver_cell_atlas_2019.notes: Human single-cell RNA-seq supports portal-central hepatocyte heterogeneity and canonical zonation markers.
human_liver_cell_atlas_2019.title: A human liver cell atlas reveals heterogeneity and epithelial progenitors

human_liver_glutathione_1980.notes: Total glutathione measured in six histologically normal human liver biopsies, reported per hepatic protein.
human_liver_glutathione_1980.title: Glutathione in human liver in normal and pathological conditions

human_liver_glycogen_mixed_meal_2000.notes: Serial in-vivo human hepatic glycogen measurements around a mixed meal.
human_liver_glycogen_mixed_meal_2000.title: Direct assessment of hepatic glycogen metabolism in humans with 13C NMR spectroscopy

human_liver_glycogen_mrs_2012.notes: In-vivo human liver tissue measurement; glycogen was quantifiable in 25 participants.
human_liver_glycogen_mrs_2012.title: Liver metabolite concentrations measured with 1H MR spectroscopy

human_liver_glycogen_overnight_1996.notes: Overnight-fasted healthy volunteers measured with localized 13C MRS.
human_liver_glycogen_overnight_1996.title: Natural abundance 13C NMR measurement of human liver glycogen

human_liver_glycogen_starvation_1973.notes: Human liver biopsy glycogen after prolonged starvation or carbohydrate-poor diet.
human_liver_glycogen_starvation_1973.title: Liver glycogen in man: the effect of total starvation or a carbohydrate-poor diet followed by carbohydrate refeeding

human_liver_multiome.notes: Human liver single-nucleus RNA plus ATAC data support joint expression/chromatin state while preserving donor and cell-type context.
human_liver_multiome.title: Liver single-nucleus multiome profiling reveals cell-type mechanisms for cardiometabolic traits

human_liver_phosphorus_mrs_2017.notes: Healthy-volunteer liver spectroscopy; reports the 1.054 kg/L liver density used for wet-tissue concentration conversion.
human_liver_phosphorus_mrs_2017.title: Phosphodiester content measured in human liver by in vivo 31P MR spectroscopy at 7 tesla

human_liver_single_cell_proteomics_2025.notes: Healthy human single-hepatocyte proteomics across 20 portal-central bins; 413 cells from 14 individuals.
human_liver_single_cell_proteomics_2025.title: Single cell spatial proteomics maps human liver zonation patterns and their vulnerability to fibrosis

human_liver_spatial_atlas_2026.notes: Human spatial transcriptomics, MERFISH and protein validation; reports species-specific portal-central expression programs.
human_liver_spatial_atlas_2026.title: A spatial atlas of the healthy human liver from live donors

human_mixed_meal_homeostasis_1996.notes: Healthy human in-vivo serial liver glycogen, hepatic glucose output and pathway-contribution measurements after a mixed meal.
human_mixed_meal_homeostasis_1996.title: Direct assessment of liver glycogen storage by 13C NMR and regulation of glucose homeostasis after a mixed meal

human_mrp2_abundance_2012.notes: Targeted LC-MS/MS of liver membrane fractions from 51 human donors.
human_mrp2_abundance_2012.title: Interindividual variability in hepatic expression of the multidrug resistance-associated protein 2 (MRP2/ABCC2)

human_ntcp_uptake_2003.notes: Taurocholate uptake kinetics in fresh and cryopreserved human hepatocytes; cryopreservation caused large activity variability.
human_ntcp_uptake_2003.title: Function of uptake transporters for taurocholate and estradiol 17beta-D-glucuronide in cryopreserved human hepatocytes

human_tki_bile_acid_trajectory.notes: Human SCHH data distinguish intracellular from extracellular bile-acid responses and show drug-specific CYP7A1 feedback; not a generic BSEP-knockout calibration.
human_tki_bile_acid_trajectory.title: Novel Bile Acid-Dependent Mechanisms of Hepatotoxicity Associated with Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitors

ncbi_gene_records.notes: Coordinates use annotation release RS_2025_08 on GCF_000001405.40.
ncbi_gene_records.title: NCBI Gene, Homo sapiens GRCh38.p14 annotation

ncbi_grch38_p14.notes: GRC/NCBI chromosome lengths and RefSeq accessions. Chromosome lengths are placed-scaffold lengths including estimated gaps.
ncbi_grch38_p14.title: Human Genome Assembly GRCh38.p14

ncbi_mtdna_rcrs.notes: Revised Cambridge Reference Sequence; 16,569 bp reference molecule.
ncbi_mtdna_rcrs.title: Homo sapiens mitochondrion, complete genome (NC_012920.1)

phh_bile_acid_gene_regulation.notes: Primary human hepatocytes support FXR-SHP and FXR-FGF19 activation, CYP7A1 repression and BSEP induction; dose/time observations must not be converted into unsourced kinetic constants.
phh_bile_acid_gene_regulation.title: Potency of individual bile acids to regulate bile acid synthesis and transport genes in primary human hepatocyte cultures

primary_hepatocyte_isolation_metabolism_.notes: Tracks energetic and redox metabolites from in-situ liver through hepatocyte isolation and culture; isolation substantially changes ATP, NADP(H), GSH and GSSG.
primary_hepatocyte_isolation_metabolism_.title: From in vivo to in vitro: Major metabolic alterations take place in hepatocytes during and following isolation

primary_hepatocyte_protein_turnover.notes: Primary human hepatocyte proteins have widely different half-lives; no universal protein-decay constant is defensible.
primary_hepatocyte_protein_turnover.title: Systematic analysis of protein turnover in primary cells

upr_proteostasis.notes: ER stress activates UPR; unresolved stress can transition from adaptation to pro-apoptotic response.
upr_proteostasis.title: The involvement of ER stress in bile acid-induced hepatocellular injury

experiment.notes: Intervention type is explicit. Control values are exact loss-of-function (0) or reference (1). Intermediate surface activity requires matched measurement or calibration.

events.notes: Functional experiment changes activity without inventing a variant coordinate, RNA count or total-protein change.

ABCB11.notes: Protein count is an order-of-magnitude total-abundance anchor; functional scale 1 is the normalized healthy reference, not a measured surface fraction.

ABCC2.notes: Protein count is an order-of-magnitude total-abundance anchor; functional scale 1 is the normalized healthy reference, not a measured surface fraction.

CYP7A1.notes: Gene-specific RNA counts, promoter kinetics and protein abundance are not calibrated.

HNF4A.notes: Gene-specific RNA counts, promoter kinetics and protein abundance are not calibrated.

NR0B2.notes: Gene-specific RNA counts, promoter kinetics and protein abundance are not calibrated.

NR1H4.notes: Gene-specific RNA counts, promoter kinetics and protein abundance are not calibrated.

SLC10A1.notes: Protein count is an order-of-magnitude total-abundance anchor; functional scale 1 is the normalized healthy reference, not a measured surface fraction.

gene_expression.notes: Seven-gene vertical slice. Missing values remain null and generate no stochastic event.

regulatory_edges.mechanism: Bile acids act as FXR ligands.

regulatory_edges.biological_context: human liver-derived and primary hepatocyte evidence
regulatory_edges.mechanism: Ligand-activated human FXR induces SHP expression.
SHP interferes with positive nuclear-receptor control of CYP7A1.
regulatory_edges.notes: FGF19/FGFR4/JNK is a parallel and potentially dominant human pathway; this edge cannot stand in for the complete feedback system.

regulatory_edges.biological_context: primary human hepatocyte and human liver-derived evidence
regulatory_edges.mechanism: HNF4A is a major positive transcriptional regulator of CYP7A1.
FGF19/FGFR4 signalling and JNK/c-Jun repress human CYP7A1 transcription.
regulatory_edges.notes: FGF19, FGFR4 and JNK are boundary nodes in milestone 2, not yet dynamic gene products in the seven-gene slice.

functional_loci.simulation_role: FXR bile-acid sensing and transcriptional regulation
SHP nuclear-receptor coregulator in bile-acid feedback

genome.notes: Reference coordinates are not an individual genotype. No inherited or somatic variant is assumed until a measured record is supplied.

ABCB11.notes: No donor-matched locus measurement is loaded; reference atlases do not define this cell's epigenetic state.

gene_modules.label: Bile-acid synthesis, sensing and transport

gene_modules.dynamic_status: only calibrated explicit genes may evolve; registry-only members are structural
gene_modules.label: Hepatocyte identity and secretion

gene_modules.dynamic_status: only calibrated explicit genes may evolve; registry-only members are structural

gene_modules.dynamic_status: only calibrated explicit genes may evolve; registry-only members are structural
gene_modules.label: Urea-cycle entry

gene_modules.dynamic_status: only calibrated explicit genes may evolve; registry-only members are structural

gene_modules.dynamic_status: only calibrated explicit genes may evolve; registry-only members are structural
gene_modules.label: DNA methylation maintenance and removal

identity.notes: Zonation labels describe the simulation context; they are not inferred from a donor expression profile.

milestones.title: Reference genome to functional expression slice

genomic_architecture.notes: All six software milestones are represented. Scientific validation remains per-dataset and per-parameter; missing donor data is never generated.

hepatic_flux_evidence.policy: Measured organ, splanchnic and whole-body records may validate scale-matched outputs only; no per-hepatocyte conversion is executed.

event_log.notes: Simulation lineage origin; not a claim about the donor cell's chronological birth time.
Recorded exposure/intervention. No persistent memory trace is consolidated without washout, persistence, rechallenge, or inheritance evidence.

lifecycle.notes: The integrated state engine has no active regeneration context, so the mature cell remains G0. Stress evidence alone does not establish senescence.

history.notes: Events are always recordable. Persistent memory traces require an explicit event-to-substrate rule and persistence evidence; unknown kinetics stay unknown.

glucose.notes: Midpoint of the curated fasting plasma range; used only at the sinusoidal boundary.

sinusoid_boundary.notes: No transport rate is inferred for unavailable species; their blood validation remains blocked.

unavailable.reason: no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary
no explicit blood pool connected to a source-backed transport boundary

membrane_state.notes: membrane_ca_brian2_boundary_v1; deterministic ODE fallback used unless Brian2 backend is attached.

metabolic_fluxes.notes: mitochondrial ATP with ROS side load
CYP-like detox consumes redox reserve and adds ROS
Disabled unless both a calibrated CYP7A1 functional scale and an explicit bile-synthesis rate are supplied; no default rate is invented
Mass-conserving intracellular-to-canalicular BSEP transfer; absolute base rate remains an existing normalized model rate
Mass-conserving intracellular-to-canalicular MRP2 transfer; absolute base rate remains an existing normalized model rate

model_authority.policy: quantitative_state wins on overlapping species; relative 0-1 state may drive visualization but cannot support quantitative biological claims.

basal_hepatic_glucose_output.evidence: reported basal healthy-human hepatic glucose output

limitations: This is an organ-level validation trajectory, not a per-cell mechanistic flux.
The cohort-average synthesis rate is not assumed constant outside the measured baseline-to-peak interval.
The trajectory is shared across zone contexts until human zone-resolved flux measurements are supplied.
Reported complete glucose-output suppression is retained categorically because no zero-valued cellular flux was measured.

blockers: No matched hepatocyte number or active parenchymal fraction for the measured liver trajectory.
No matched zone contribution to whole-liver glycogen synthesis.
No matched adult healthy-PHH GLUT2 bidirectional transport capacity.
No donor-matched insulin/glucagon concentration trajectory is loaded.

anchors.applicability: Checks the order of magnitude of the hepatocyte albumin protein pool.
anchors.assay: LC-MS/MS with Total Protein Approach and Proteomic Ruler
anchors.biological_system: purified primary human hepatocytes from surgical liver resections
anchors.limitations: Average across donors; does not separate intracellular, secretory-pathway and newly secreted albumin, and is not a synthesis rate.

anchors.applicability: Constrains total BSEP protein abundance in a healthy human-hepatocyte reference cohort.
anchors.biological_system: purified primary human hepatocytes from surgical liver resections
anchors.limitations: Does not provide canalicular surface fraction, total protein mass per modeled cell, or transport-active copies.

anchors.applicability: Constrains total membrane-fraction MRP2 abundance and its donor variability.
anchors.limitations: Tissue membrane denominator includes multiple membrane pools; canalicular surface fraction and copies per hepatocyte are not measured.

anchors.applicability: Provides an in-vivo healthy-liver glycogen distribution target.
anchors.limitations: Tissue wet-weight concentration is not identical to cytosolic concentration in one hepatocyte and feeding state was not a matched simulation input.

anchors.applicability: Independent measured affinity anchor for the BSEP taurocholate rate law.
anchors.biological_system: recombinant human BSEP in inverted Sf9 membrane vesicles
anchors.limitations: Recombinant vesicle affinity is assay-specific; it is not a whole-hepatocyte flux parameter.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the source assay curve when the same assay denominator is represented.
anchors.biological_system: recombinant human BSEP in inverted Sf9 membrane vesicles
anchors.limitations: Cannot be converted to hepatocyte molecules per second without matched active surface copies or matched membrane-protein mass.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the human-hepatocyte taurocholate uptake affinity range.
anchors.limitations: Measured uptake includes intact-cell context and potentially additional uptake transporters; cryopreserved activity ranged from 10-200% of fresh cells.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the tissue-equivalent ATP pool and adenylate energy charge.
anchors.limitations: Total tissue ATP is not a compartment-resolved free cytosolic ATP measurement in isolated PHH.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the tissue-equivalent ADP pool and adenylate energy charge.
anchors.limitations: Total tissue ADP is not compartment resolved.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the tissue-equivalent AMP pool and adenylate energy charge.
anchors.limitations: Total tissue AMP is not compartment resolved.

anchors.applicability: Validates consistency of the initialized ATP, ADP and AMP pools.
anchors.limitations: Tissue-average energy charge does not resolve hepatocyte zones or organelle compartments.
Does not provide NADH or cytosolic-versus-mitochondrial allocation.

anchors.applicability: Calibrates apparent ATP turnover around the healthy baseline pools.
anchors.limitations: Magnetization-transfer exchange is not identical to net mitochondrial ATP synthesis.

anchors.biological_system: healthy human liver in vivo after a mixed meal
anchors.limitations: Whole-liver concentration is used as a tissue-equivalent lumped hepatocyte pool.

anchors.biological_system: healthy human liver in vivo after overnight fast
anchors.limitations: Whole-liver concentration is used as a tissue-equivalent lumped hepatocyte pool.

anchors.biological_system: human liver after total starvation or carbohydrate-poor diet
anchors.limitations: The model uses the range midpoint and retains a tissue-mass-equivalent interpretation.

anchors.applicability: Constrains the total hepatic glutathione pool.
anchors.limitations: Conversion to a model concentration requires an explicit hepatic protein-density assumption and does not resolve GSH from GSSG compartments.

anchors.applicability: Sets the first-order replacement timescale of the effective sinusoidal concentration boundary.
anchors.assay: dynamic tracer imaging with physiological compartment model
anchors.limitations: Whole-liver mean transit time does not resolve one sinusoid, acinar zone or donor-specific perfusion.

anchors.applicability: Defines the postabsorptive glucose concentration range at the blood-facing model boundary.
anchors.limitations: Systemic plasma is used as the effective sinusoidal inlet target; portal-arterial mixing and donor variation are not resolved.

phh_baseline.applicability: Healthy human liver tissue-equivalent baseline for a lumped hepatocyte model; not donor-matched isolated PHH cytosol.
phh_baseline.policy: Measurements retain their original assay denominator and biological context. None is converted to a whole-cell initial value or flux without the missing matched conversion measurement.

ADP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

AMP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

ATP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

NAD_plus.notes: Total tissue NAD+; no cytosol/matrix split is inferred.

glycogen.notes: Peak after a mixed meal; bounds are mean +/- SEM.

ADP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

AMP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

ATP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

NAD_plus.notes: Total tissue NAD+; no cytosol/matrix split is inferred.

glucose_blood.notes: Midpoint of the curated fasting plasma range; used only at the sinusoidal boundary.

glycogen.notes: Healthy volunteers without carbohydrate preparation; bounds are mean +/- SEM.

ADP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

AMP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

ATP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

NAD_plus.notes: Total tissue NAD+; no cytosol/matrix split is inferred.

glycogen.notes: Midpoint of the reported biopsy range; tissue-mass denominator retained as an effective lumped-model value.

blocking_measurements: per-cell total protein and membrane-protein mass for the modeled cell
canalicular surface-localized BSEP and MRP2 copy numbers

scientific_release.authoritative_scope: Healthy PHH metabolic pools, nutrition-state glycogen, apparent ATP turnover, human zonation reference context, postabsorptive blood-glucose perfusion boundary, and healthy-human mixed-meal organ trajectory only. Blood-to-cell, per-cell and zone-specific flux remain blocked.

blockers: per-cell total protein and membrane-protein mass for the modeled cell
canalicular surface-localized BSEP and MRP2 copy numbers
NADH and GSH/GSSG are not compartment resolved
healthy donor trajectory validation is not complete
checks: source-traceable metabolic pool initialization
human-liver-anchored apparent ATP turnover
unified quantitative PHH state excludes relative schematic units
human-specific zonation context cannot apply unmeasured flux or oxygen scaling
sinusoid v2 enables sourced perfusion while uncalibrated cell and zonal fluxes fail closed
human mixed-meal trajectory is retained at organ scale and cannot invent per-cell flux

limitations: Tissue-equivalent pools are not compartment-resolved isolated-PHH measurements.
Effective molecule counts use the lumped model cytosol and are not direct copy-number measurements.
This baseline is static; source-backed substrate transport and redox dynamics remain blocked.

ADP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

AMP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

ATP.notes: Control human-liver HPLC mean converted with 1.054 kg/L liver density.

NAD_plus.notes: Total tissue NAD+; no cytosol/matrix split is inferred.

glucose_blood.notes: Midpoint of the curated fasting plasma range; used only at the sinusoidal boundary.

glycogen.notes: Healthy volunteers without carbohydrate preparation; bounds are mean +/- SEM.

timing_profile.notes: No universal timing profile is encoded for this context.

scientific_audit.policy: Only source-backed surfaces may drive scientific validation. Schematic and blocked surfaces may drive visualization but not quantitative claims.

surfaces.limitations: Whole-liver-equivalent values are not compartment-resolved isolated-PHH measurements.
Systemic plasma target and whole-liver transit time do not resolve portal/arterial mixing or one sinusoid.

surfaces.action: retain categorical marker direction; block numeric flux scaling
surfaces.limitations: Human atlas marker directions are not donor-specific expression values; zonal pO2 and reaction-rate effect sizes remain unavailable.

surfaces.action: retain perfusion relaxation; block blood-cell and zonal flux
surfaces.limitations: Whole-liver transit and systemic fasting glucose do not identify one sinusoid volume, GLUT2 exchange capacity or zone-specific cellular flux.

surfaces.action: retain measured trajectory; prohibit per-cell allocation
surfaces.limitations: Healthy-human mixed-meal glycogen and hepatic-output observations are whole-liver cohort averages, not single-cell or zone-resolved fluxes.

surfaces.action: use only for scale-matched validation; never initialize per-cell flux
surfaces.limitations: Thirty-one literature records span organ, splanchnic and whole-body scopes; portal-resolved healthy and single-hepatocyte flux are unavailable.

surfaces.action: retain only as qualitative renderer driver
surfaces.limitations: Cycle rates, error fractions, damage and repair coefficients are uncalibrated.
Former per-hour base risks and stress weights were project assumptions.
Binucleated-cell prevalence cannot be used as a per-division failure probability.

surfaces.action: require active surface copies and matched turnover
surfaces.limitations: Total or membrane-fraction abundance is not active canalicular surface abundance.
Legacy GPx, reductase, PPP and ROS influx rates are placeholders; isolation changes these pools.

surfaces.action: replace pseudo-first-order magnitudes with fitted human fluxes
surfaces.limitations: Topology and stoichiometry are useful, but several absolute rates are illustrative.
Relative stress thresholds are not matched human-hepatocyte commitment kinetics.

surfaces.action: run dynamics only for calibrated genes
surfaces.limitations: Reference coordinates are real; most donor expression and gene-specific kinetics are unknown.

coupling_edges.blocker: Requires matched human PHH GLUT2 surface abundance and bidirectional transport capacity.
Requires human zone-resolved glucose uptake/release flux, not transcript direction alone.

limitations: The 13.4 s value is a whole-liver mean transit time, not one sinusoid residence time.
Boundary recovery is perfusion homeostasis; it does not claim hepatocyte glucose uptake.
No mouse spatial-metabolomics effect size is transferred into the human model.
No anatomical sinusoid control volume or human zonal pO2 is assigned.
Marker direction is human-atlas evidence; no donor-specific expression value is inferred.
Relative oxygen context is directional only; no human zonal pO2 value is assigned.
Zonation does not scale reaction rates until matched human quantitative effects are loaded.
